source_code:
# source: https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/19406
# repo: sgl-project/sglang
# title: [Bug] Cannot load Qwen3.5-27B AWQ/INT8: Marlin repack alignment error (`size_n` not divisible by 64) & Mamba cache dtype mismatch
# 
# ### Checklist
# - [x] I searched related issues but found no solution.
# - [x] The bug persists in the latest version.
# - [x] Issues without environment info and a minimal reproducible demo are hard to resolve and may receive no feedback.
# - [x] If this is not a bug report but a general question, please start a discussion at https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/discussions. Otherwise, it will be closed.
# - [x] Please use English. Otherwise, it will be closed.
# ### Describe the bug
# **Describe the bug**
# Currently, loading the AWQ or INT8 quantized versions of `Qwen3.5-27B` fails entirely on the latest SGLang `main` branch. 
# > https://huggingface.co/cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ-4bit
# Multiple users (myself and others on Hugging Face) are experiencing the exact same chain of crashes when attempting to

**Issue 2: Mamba `causal_conv1d_fwd` Dtype Mismatch (When bypassing Marlin)**
To workaround the Marlin crash, I modified `config.json` to force `"quant_method": "awq"` and launched with `--quantization awq --dtype float16` (since Triton AWQ doesn't support bfloat16).
The model weights load successfully, but it crashes on the very first token generation.

*Root cause of Issue 2:* Qwen3.5 has a nested `"text_config": {"dtype": "bfloat16"}` in its `config.json`. The Mamba SSM initialization ignores the CLI `--dtype float16` flag and allocates `conv_states_` in `bfloat16`. When `sgl_kernel.causal_conv1d_fwd` is called, it receives a `float16` input but a `bfloat16` state cache, triggering a hard assert.

*Error Log 2:*
expected_fix_hint: workaround the Marlin crash, I modified `config.json` to force `"quant_method": "awq"` and launched with `--quantization awq --dtype float16` (since Triton AWQ doesn't support bfloat16).
